meaning_english: To agree to a demand or request, or to assume a position of power. Synonyms: Acquiesce, Assent, Inherit.
To confuse or muddle a situation completely. Synonyms: Botch, Bungle, Mess up.
To reprimand someone severely. Synonyms: Chastise, Upbraid, Berate.
To speak or write against something with great force and passion. Synonyms: Inveigh, Rail, Fulminate.
To originate or issue forth from a source. Synonyms: Originate, Exude, Radiate.
To understand a difficult problem or person after much thought. Synonyms: Comprehend, Decipher.
To deny or contradict a fact or statement. Synonyms: Deny, Contradict, Dispute.
To talk or complain about something repeatedly and annoyingly. Synonyms: Dwell on, Reiterate.
To have an effect on something, often negative, or to encroach. Synonyms: Encroach, Infringe, Affect.
To be in accord or consistent with something. Synonyms: Match, Coincide, Agree.
To fall over suddenly, often due to illness or exhaustion. Synonyms: Faint, Collapse.
To accept an opportunity eagerly. Synonyms: Seize, Grab, Embrace.
To show that someone or something is different from others. Synonyms: Characterize, Distinguish.
To cancel or refuse to accept a plan or idea. Synonyms: Veto, Reject, Cancel.
When a thought or idea comes into someone's mind. Synonyms: Dawn on, Strike.
To reduce something in size or amount, especially to its essential parts. Synonyms: Trim, Diminish.
To align or match perfectly with a set of data or facts. Synonyms: Conform, Square with.
To complain angrily about something. Synonyms: Fulminate, Inveigh, Protest.
To burden someone with an unwanted task or responsibility. Synonyms: Encumber, Burden.
To treat someone or something without seriousness or respect. Synonyms: Toy with, Dally.
To experience something, especially something unpleasant or a process. Synonyms: Endure, Experience.
To express confidence in the truth or reliability of someone/something. Synonyms: Attest, Guarantee.
To wait until an unpleasant event or situation is over. Synonyms: Endure, Wait.
To delete or cross out a specific piece of information. Synonyms: Strike out, Delete.
To give up possession of something or to reveal a secret. Synonyms: Relinquish, Surrender.
To stop paying attention and start daydreaming. Synonyms: Space out, Daydream.
To ask many people the same question. Synonyms: Inquire.
To base your hopes on something. Synonyms: Rely on, Depend.
To refuse to speak or share information. Synonyms: Go silent.
To think about something negative for a long period. Synonyms: Harp on.
